name: Proton VPN
tagline: Swiss-based, open-source, with a genuinely usable free tier.

logging.summary: No-logs policy independently verified by five consecutive Securitum audits, including physical inspection of live servers in Zürich (Aug 2025).

transparency.realWorldNoLog: Switzerland sits outside the 5/9/14 Eyes and has no VPN data-retention requirement; Proton publishes a transparency report.

bestFor: People who want a trustworthy free tier
notFor: Fully anonymous signup (an email or username is required)
pros: All apps open source and independently audited
Unlimited free tier with no data cap (1 device, 5 countries)
Stealth obfuscation protocol and Secure Core multi-hop
cons: Signup requires an email or username (not fully anonymous)
One company spanning many products is a broad attack surface
notes: Overseen by the non-profit Proton Foundation; the free tier is funded by paid subscriptions.

sources.label: Tom's Guide — fourth consecutive no-logs audit
